title: Test failure: urlencoded parser returns {"0":"val1","1":"val2"} instead of ["val1","val2"] for duplicate keys

comments.body: I encountered the same issue when upgrading to body-parser 2.x. Found a great explanation of the breaking changes here: https://expressguides.io/body-parser-duplicate-keys-handling/ - turns out the querystring module behavior shifted between Node versions and that's what caused the numeric key objects instead of arrays.

body:
## Environment information

**Version**:

express: 5.2.1
body-parser: ^2.2.1

**Platform**:

Darwin 25.1.0 (macOS)

**Node.js version**:

>= 18 (as specified in package.json engines)

**Any other relevant information**:
- Test framework: Mocha ^10.7.3
- Testing library: Supertest ^6.3.0
- This appears to be related to body-parser 2.x behavior changes

## What steps will reproduce the bug?

1. Run the test suite:

npm test

2. Or run the specific failing test:

npm test -- --grep "should parse multiple key instances"

## Expected behavior

When parsing URL-encoded data with duplicate keys using express.urlencoded({ extended: false }), the parser should return an array:

**Request:**
POST / HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

user=Tobi&user=Loki

**Expected response:**
{"user":["Tobi","Loki"]}

## Actual behavior

The parser returns an object with numeric keys instead of an array:

**Actual response:**
{"user":{"0":"Tobi","1":"Loki"}}

## Test failure output

```
express.urlencoded()
       with extended option
         when false
           should parse multiple key instances:
     Error: expected '{"user":["Tobi","Loki"]}' response body, got '{"user":{"0":"Tobi","1":"Loki"}}'
      at Context.<anonymous> (test/express.urlencoded.js:106:12)
```

## Affected test

File: test/express.urlencoded.js, lines 101-107

```
it('should parse multiple key instances', function (done) {
  request(this.app)
    .post('/')
    .set('Content-Type', 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded')
    .send('user=Tobi&user=Loki')
    .expect(200, '{"user":["Tobi","Loki"]}', done)
})
```
## Possible cause

This appears to be a breaking change in body-parser 2.x regarding how duplicate keys in URL-encoded data are handled when extended: false. The simple querystring parser may have changed its behavior to return objects with numeric keys instead of arrays.

## Questions

1. Is this the intended behavior for body-parser 2.x?
2. Should the test expectation be updated to match the new behavior?
3. Or is this a regression that needs to be fixed in body-parser?
